summary: The script is syntactically valid and generally well-structured, but it does not correctly implement the specified data interfaces, item content loading, trait ranges, or output merging relative to the provided experimental design. Several logic/implementation mismatches with the task description and dataset schema are present, though imports, error handling, and performance are mostly fine.

issues.description: Personas are not loaded from the specified synthetic_personas.csv schema (with demographic and trait columns) and instead a fallback synthetic format with different column names is used. This breaks alignment with the task specification and prevents using the provided 470-row dataset as described.
issues.solution: Remove or gate the fallback generator for synthetic_personas.csv. Implement loading from the real schema under data_fitting/persona_trait_experiment/synthetic_personas.csv, ensuring columns: Country, Employment Status, Occupation_cat, sex, Highest education level completed, Ethnicity Simplified, Age, CRT2_Total_adj, BCRT_Total, CRT_Combined, NFC_Total, BFI_Extraversion, BFI_Agreeableness, BFI_Conscientiousness, BFI_Neuroticism, BFI_Openness, BFI_Attn_Pass. Update Persona.from_series to: (1) accept this schema, (2) derive persona_id (e.g., index-based or a dedicated id column), (3) map BFI_Extraversion → bfi_extraversion, etc., and NFC_Total → nfc_score, CRT2_Total_adj or CRT_Combined → crt2_score. Ensure the simulator uses these mapped traits for prompt construction and scoring.

issues.description: The code does not load or use the YAML psychometric specification files (crt2_items.yaml, bcrt_items.yaml, nfc18_items.yaml, bfi10_items.yaml, writing_tasks.yaml) and instead hardcodes shorter test definitions and ad hoc writing prompts. This diverges from the required configuration-driven design.
issues.location: Section 'Psychometric tests definitions' (build_bfi_brief_test, build_nfc18_short_test, build_crt2_test, build_bcrt_test) and writing_tasks list inside Simulator.rollout()
issues.solution: Replace hardcoded TestItem definitions and writing_tasks list with YAML-driven loading. Implement loaders that read the YAML files from data_fitting/persona_trait_experiment/, parse id/name/items/instructions/response_scale, and construct Test/TestItem objects accordingly. For BFI, NFC, CRT2, and bCRT, use the exact item texts and IDs from the YAML. For writing tasks, load writing_tasks.yaml and use its IDs and prompts. This ensures that administering, shuffling, and scoring are aligned with the provided instruments.

issues.description: Trait ranges and scales are not aligned with the provided dataset: Persona.from_series enforces bfi_* and nfc_score in [1,5] and crt2_score in [0,4], but the real data has NFC_Total and CRT2_Total_adj as continuous scores with different ranges and semantics. Additionally, NFC Likert responses are generated on a 1–7 scale while the NFC-18 specification is 1–5.
issues.solution: Adjust trait ingestion to: (1) accept the ranges in synthetic_personas.csv (e.g., NFC_Total approximately 1–5, CRT2_Total_adj as a continuous measure mapped onto the 0–4 level via thresholds or quantiles), and (2) normalize/transform as needed before mapping to 1–9 qualifier levels. For NFC item responses, constrain Likert responses to a 1–5 scale to match nfc18_items.yaml.response_scale and reverse-score using 6 - resp instead of 8 - resp. Remove hard-coded CRT2 integer range checks and instead clamp mapped levels after an explicit mapping from CRT2_Total_adj or CRT_Combined.

issues.description: Simulation outputs are not merged with the original persona rows from synthetic_personas.csv as required; instead, they are merged with whichever dataframe is passed in as valid_df, which in the current design is a synthetic or altered schema. Furthermore, persona_id is assumed to exist but is not part of the provided CSV schema.
issues.location: Simulator.rollout() (loop over valid_df, construction of persona_record and raw_records)
issues.solution: Ensure that valid_df is the true validation split of the real synthetic_personas dataframe and that persona_id is either: (a) a dedicated ID column added upon load, or (b) the original index converted to an ID. Merge simulation outputs with the full original persona row via that ID. Explicitly include all original CSV columns in the final sim_df and JSON records, so the resulting dataset is analysis-ready and consistent with the specification.

issues.description: The BFI, NFC, and CRT tests used in the simulation do not match the specified instruments: BFI-brief is redefined as 10 custom items instead of using the provided 8-item BFI-brief YAML; NFC is shortened to 6 items and uses 1–7 scale instead of the full 18 items with a 1–5 scale; CRT2 and bCRT items differ from crt2_items.yaml and bcrt_items.yaml.
issues.solution: Reimplement these builders as thin wrappers over the YAML configurations: (1) parse bfi10_items.yaml and construct items using the provided texts, IDs, and response_scale (1–5), mapping items to the correct Big Five domain and reverse-keying rules consistent with the brief BFI scoring; (2) parse nfc18_items.yaml and build a full 18-item test with correct key direction (p/a) and 1–5 Likert scale; (3) parse crt2_items.yaml and bcrt_items.yaml and ensure correct answers are either provided in YAML or attached by a separate scoring key; (4) maintain stable item IDs from YAML for downstream analysis.

issues.description: Persona prompt construction ignores the real demographics and specific textual descriptors in synthetic_personas.csv (e.g., country, occupation, education, ethnicity) and the qualifier scale mapping from 1–5 scores to 1–9 levels for BFI and NFC based on the synthetic dataset. The current code uses a different schema and only uses limited demographic fields from the fallback data.
issues.location: Persona.from_series() and Persona.build_full_persona_prompt() plus demographic selection in Persona.from_series
issues.solution: Adapt Persona.from_series to build demographics from the real columns (Country, Employment Status, Occupation_cat, sex, Highest education level completed, Ethnicity Simplified, Age) and pass them into MemoryAgent. Ensure build_bfi_granular_prompt and build_nfc_prompt: (a) use level = 2*(score-1)+1 mapping explicitly on BFI_* and NFC_Total, (b) pick descriptors from serapio_goldberg_markers.csv and NFC descriptor lists according to the 1–9 qualifier scale as described, and (c) construct prompts conforming to the templates in the task: BFI granular_serapio, BFI coarse_numeric, BFI coarse_descriptive, NFC granular_9_level, CRT levels_0_to_4.

issues.description: The `admin_mode` and shuffling logic is implemented only conceptually. While num_api_calls reflects per_item/per_test/all_tests, all structured tests are simulated locally, and there is no actual prompt grouping that corresponds to test administration; this may be acceptable under the 'pseudo-LLM' note, but from an experimental-control standpoint, there is no explicit representation of item order, block order, or combined prompts that would be used if migrating to real LLM calls.
issues.solution: Even if responses are simulated locally, explicitly structure the 'prompt blocks' to mimic true API usage: e.g., build a prompt object per call that includes: persona_prompt + test instructions + item(s) (or all tests). Store this 'prompt schema' in the JSON under raw_responses for each call, and increment num_api_calls per such block. Ensure shuffle_items_within_tests and shuffle_test_order alter the order in which these blocks and items are assembled, matching the administration_modes and shuffling_options spec.

issues.description: Path handling does not respect the specified data_fitting/persona_trait_experiment/ subdirectory; instead, DATA_DIR is set to PROJECT_ROOT/DATA_PATH (default 'data').
issues.location: Global constants PROJECT_ROOT, DATA_PATH, DATA_DIR and load_data()
issues.solution: Change DATA_DIR to point to the required folder, e.g., DATA_DIR = os.path.join(PROJECT_ROOT, 'data_fitting', 'persona_trait_experiment'). Optionally, add a CLI flag or environment variable for this, but default should match the task description. Update load_data() and YAML loaders to use this directory.

issues.description: Cronbach’s alpha is computed across all BFI and NFC item-level columns combined, without separating subscales. This yields an overall reliability for a heterogeneous item set rather than per-scale estimates as implied by the spec.
issues.solution: Optionally enhance reliability computation by: (1) grouping BFI items by scale (extraversion, agreeableness, etc.) and computing alpha per domain, and (2) computing NFC alpha on the full 18-item set once the complete instrument is used. Document clearly what alpha refers to (overall vs per-scale).

issues.description: The code raises errors if openai is missing only when making the call, which is appropriate, but Calibrator.fit and load_data assume certain columns ('bfi_*', 'nfc_score', 'crt2_score') that may not exist if the real CSV schema is later wired in; this will raise ValueError without graceful guidance to the user.
issues.solution: Once the real schema is wired, update Calibrator.fit to look for the real column names and add clear error messages if they are missing. Consider providing a schema check at load time that validates presence of required columns and logs a descriptive message instead of relying on ValueErrors at deeper stages.

issues.description: The simulation runs all personas sequentially and, for each writing task, may call the external LLM. For the current N (~470) and 2 writing tasks, this is acceptable, but there is no rate limiting or batch handling.
issues.solution: If scaling up, consider: (1) adding configurable sampling of personas or tasks, (2) implementing rate limiting or concurrency controls around LLM calls, and (3) allowing a toggle to disable LLM usage (pure heuristic mode) via CLI to save cost and time. For now, with N≈470 and 2 tasks, performance is likely acceptable.

issues.description: The main() function is called directly at global scope, which is intentionally required for the sandbox but non-idiomatic in standard Python scripts.
issues.solution: No change needed for this system, as the special requirement explicitly mandates a direct main() call instead of an if __name__ == '__main__' guard. Just be aware this will execute main() on import in other contexts.
